summary.files_audited: 2372 HTML pages, 1000+ JS files, 150+ CSS files, shared assets, teacher tools

test_results.pedagogy_check: PASS (1041 pass, 24 warnings, 0 failures) — 24 Spanish A1 grammar chapters missing common-mistakes trap-row slides (expected, per TECH_DEBT M4)
test_results.check_links: 243 broken links — all in fr/b1 grammar hubs (dead links to non-existent slides/game/worksheet files); Spanish/English tracks clean
test_results.check_structure: 72 structure issues — all Spanish vocab flashcard breadcrumb errors (72 es/* flashcards.html missing crumbs); expected per TECH_DEBT M2
test_results.html_semantics: CLEAN — profile.html uses semantic <button> throughout, no div onclick or inline event handlers found site-wide
test_results.css_variables: CLEAN — all accent colors use var(--amber), hardcoded hex only in CSS var definitions and intentional exceptions (game green #2E7D52, print grey #6B6B6B, certificate amber)
test_results.cache_bust: GOOD — shared assets pinned consistently (base.css v123, dark-init.js v109, mascot.js/css v2) across all consumers
test_results.console_logs: 4 console.error, 1 console.warn in mascot.js + 1 in store.js — all error handling, no debug logs

themes_found: Minor console statements are error handlers (acceptable)
Spanish content stubs and French missing content are tracked roadmap items, not bugs
HTML markup quality is excellent — semantic buttons, no inline handlers, no duplicate IDs
Design system compliance is 100% on accent color usage after H1 batch fix
Two legacy one-off scripts worth archiving
Small opportunities to harden CI/CD and tighten version management

proposals.title: Remove error-handler console.log from production code (mascot.js, store.js)
proposals.impact: Reduces noise in production DevTools; console should only show user-facing errors when genuinely critical
proposals.description: mascot.js has 5 console.error/warn calls and store.js has 1 console.warn for error handling. These help during dev but should be removed or wrapped in debug-only guards for production. Suggest replacing with optional error reporting hook or removing entirely since failures are already caught.
proposals.success_metric: Zero console.* statements in production files (except optional error reporting hook if added)
proposals.notes: These are currently wrapped in try-catch, so they're defensive. Removing them is safe; alternatively wrap in `if (window.DEBUG)` guard. Test mascot.js animations after change.

proposals.title: Consolidate Spanish vocab flashcard breadcrumbs (es/* flashcards.html missing crumbs)
proposals.impact: Fixes 72 structure validation failures; improves user navigation consistency
proposals.description: 72 Spanish vocabulary flashcard pages (es/a1-c2/vocabulary/*/flashcards.html) are missing breadcrumb HTML. This is tracked as TECH_DEBT M2 and shows as MISSING CRUMB errors in check_structure output. Add breadcrumb block (matching English pattern) to all es/* flashcard files.
proposals.success_metric: python3 scripts/check_structure.py returns 0 structure issues; all es/ flashcard breadcrumbs render correctly
proposals.estimated_effort: 30 minutes (script-based batch edit, test on 3 samples first)
proposals.notes: Use Python script to match template, not sed/awk per CLAUDE.md. Verify breadcrumb path structure matches es/ hierarchy before batch apply. Do 3-file test first.

proposals.title: Fix French B1+ grammar hub dead links (fr/b1+ slides/game/worksheet paths missing)
proposals.impact: Unblocks 243 broken links; allows French roadmap #4 content generation to proceed with valid link structure
proposals.description: 81 French navigation hubs exist (fr/*/grammaire/*/index.html) but link to non-existent slides.html, game.html, worksheet.html files. These show as 243 BROKEN links (all in fr/b1 grammaire). Two options: (a) create placeholder stubs for all 81 chapters, or (b) conditionally hide links until content exists. Recommend (a) for consistency with existing Spanish placeholders.
proposals.success_metric: python3 scripts/check_links.py shows 0 broken links in fr/ section; French hubs validate structurally
proposals.estimated_effort: 45 minutes (create stub templates, batch generate for 81 chapters)
proposals.notes: Check fr/ hub index templates first to understand structure. Stubs should include the standard placeholder 'Content coming soon' pattern. Run link checker on fr/ subset after batch creation.

proposals.title: Audit and archive legacy one-off scripts (fix_*, migrate_*, gen_*)
proposals.impact: Reduces repo clutter; makes it clear which scripts are actively maintained vs. historical
proposals.description: TECH_DEBT L8 notes ~19 one-off migration/generator scripts (fix_*, migrate_*, gen_es_*, gen_en_*) linger beside reusable validators (check_links.py, pedagogy_check.py, etc.). Audit which are still needed vs. dead code, then move unused ones to scripts/archive/ or delete if truly one-time.
files_affected: scripts/*.py (audit all fix_* and migrate_* files)
proposals.success_metric: scripts/ directory contains only maintained validators + 1-2 essential builders; legacy scripts moved to archive/ or removed
proposals.notes: Do a grep search for any external references to these scripts before deleting. Check git history (git log --oneline -- scripts/) to confirm they're not actively used.

proposals.title: Pin shared asset versions in .env or config to reduce cache-bust coordination
proposals.impact: Single source of truth for all shared asset versions; reduces risk of version drift when updating assets
proposals.description: Currently each shared asset has its own independent ?v= parameter (base.css v123, slides.css v115, mascot.js v2, etc.). If you need to update base.css, you must grep and update all 330+ HTML consumers. Suggest a central version map (version.json or shared/.versions) that page generators or a build-time hook reads, so bumping one asset version updates all references automatically.
proposals.success_metric: When base.css version is bumped, all consumer pages automatically get the new ?v= without manual grep-and-replace
proposals.estimated_effort: 2 hours (design map schema, write update hook, test on 3 pages, document in CLAUDE.md)
proposals.notes: This requires either a static build step or a simple template injector. Since Word Play has no build system, a shell hook in .claude/hooks could do it at deploy time.

proposals.title: Add lint rule to reject hardcoded hex colors (except --amber variable definitions)
proposals.impact: Prevents future color drift; enforces design system compliance automatically
proposals.description: design-check.sh hook (per TECH_DEBT M5) currently catches emoji but not hardcoded hex colors in inline styles. A few outliers remain (certificate.html amber hardcoding, activity card type colors #2E7D52 green #6B6B6B grey — all intentional and documented). Add a linter that rejects new hex codes except in approved contexts (CSS var defs, intentional exceptions list).
proposals.success_metric: Commit hook rejects any PR adding new hardcoded hex colors outside approved list; exceptions are documented in CLAUDE.md
proposals.estimated_effort: 1 hour (enhance hook regex, test on sample files)
proposals.notes: Requires maintaining an exception list. Current intentional exceptions: #2E7D52 (game green), #6B6B6B (print grey), amber in certificates. Document why each exists.

proposals.title: Add landing-page dark-mode CSS variable fallback audit
proposals.impact: Ensures consistent dark mode rendering across all landing pages; catches any fallback variable misses
proposals.description: index.html and a/index.html use --navy, --navy-light, --muted-deep variables which have fallbacks (e.g., `var(--muted-deep,#8A96A8)`). These fallbacks should only be in CSS, never in HTML inline styles. Audit all inline style attributes to ensure they use CSS variables only, with fallbacks managed in base.css.
proposals.success_metric: Zero fallback hex values in inline style attributes; all color values use CSS variables
proposals.estimated_effort: 30 minutes (grep for style= and verify all colors use var())
proposals.notes: This is preventative; the current code is correct but worth locking down to prevent future regression.

proposals.title: Document and validate mascot.js state machine thoroughly
proposals.impact: Reduces cognitive load for future maintainers; prevents state machine bugs
proposals.description: mascot.js implements a frame-based animation state machine with 6 states (idle, fly, spell, walk, happy, sad) and localStorage persistence. The state cycle is non-obvious (IDLE → HAPPY → FLY → SPELL → WALK → SAD → IDLE). Add JSDoc comments explaining state transitions, frame durations, and the localStorage recovery logic. Verify idle state loops infinitely while others play once then revert.
proposals.success_metric: mascot.js has clear JSDoc on init(), setBeeState(), state machine, and frame animation logic; test coverage for state transitions documented
proposals.estimated_effort: 45 minutes (add JSDoc, verify test cases mentally)
proposals.notes: No functional changes needed. This is documentation + defensive testing prep in case mascot behavior needs tweaking later.
